body.context: When a user selects ticket types, a new `orders` record must be created in `pending` status and linked to one or more `order_items`.
acceptance_criteria: Authenticated user can POST with `ticket_type_id` + `quantity`
Server validates `quantity_sold < quantity_total` on `ticket_types`
Creates `orders` row with `status = pending` and correct `total_amount`
Creates one `order_items` row per ticket type with `unit_price` snapshot
Returns `order.id` and full order payload
Returns 409 if tickets are sold out

title: Order expiry job â€” auto-cancel stale pending orders

body.context: Orders left in `pending` status must expire after a configurable TTL (e.g. 15 min) to free reserved seats.
acceptance_criteria: Cron/worker runs every N minutes
Sets `orders.status = expired` for unpaid orders past TTL
Does not affect orders already in `paid` or `cancelled`
Emits an event/log entry per expired order

body.context: Provides a read endpoint for order details including nested items and current status.
acceptance_criteria: Returns order with nested `order_items` and current `status`
Only the owning user or an admin can fetch
404 if not found, 403 if not owner

body.context: Creates a `payments` record linked to an `order_id` and calls the chosen payment provider to get a checkout URL / intent.
acceptance_criteria: Accepts `order_id` and `provider` (enum)
Validates order is still in `pending` status
Creates `payments` row with `status = pending` and provider's `transaction_ref`
Returns provider redirect URL or client secret
Idempotent â€” re-calling returns the existing pending payment for the same order

title: Payment webhook handler â€” confirm or fail payment

body.context: The payment provider calls back with success/failure. This handler must atomically update `payments` and `orders` and trigger ticket issuance on success.
acceptance_criteria: Validates provider signature / HMAC before processing
On success: sets `payments.status = paid`, sets `orders.status = paid`
On failure: sets `payments.status = failed`, leaves order in `pending` (retriable)
Publishes internal `payment.succeeded` event for ticket generation
Returns HTTP 200 to provider regardless to prevent retries on already-processed events
Idempotent â€” duplicate webhooks for same `transaction_ref` are no-ops

title: Cancel order and refund â€” POST /orders/:id/cancel

body.context: Allows users or admins to cancel an order and trigger a refund if payment was already made.
acceptance_criteria: Only callable by order owner or admin
Sets `orders.status = cancelled`
If a `paid` payment exists, triggers refund via provider and updates `payments.status`
Invalidates any issued tickets for this order (`tickets.status = cancelled`)
Returns 409 if order is already cancelled/expired

body.context: On `payment.succeeded` event, generate one `tickets` row per seat purchased, each with a unique QR code.
acceptance_criteria: Listens to `payment.succeeded` event (or called synchronously from webhook handler)
For each unit in `order_items.quantity`, creates a `tickets` row with `status = active`
`ticket_code` is a UNIQUE, cryptographically random QR-safe string
Sets `tickets.issued_at` to current timestamp
Idempotent â€” re-running for the same `order_item_id` must not create duplicates

body.context: Handles QR code scanning at event gates to mark attendees as checked in.
acceptance_criteria: Accepts `ticket_code` (QR scan) and `gate`, `device_id`
Validates ticket is `active` and belongs to the correct event
Creates `event_checkins` row with `checked_in_at`
Sets `tickets.status = used`
Returns 409 if ticket already checked in

title: DB migrations â€” orders, order_items, payments, tickets, event_checkins

body.context: Create all tables required by the order and payment cycle with correct schema, constraints, and indexes.
acceptance_criteria: Migration creates all five tables with correct columns, types, and FK constraints per the ER diagram
Indexes on `orders.user_id`, `order_items.order_id`, `payments.order_id`, `tickets.ticket_code` (unique), `tickets.order_item_id`
Migration is reversible (down migration provided)

title: Integration tests â€” full order-to-ticket happy path

body.context: End-to-end integration tests covering the full order, payment, and ticket issuance cycle including edge cases.
scenarios: Create order â†’ initiate payment â†’ simulate webhook success â†’ assert tickets created
Create order â†’ let it expire â†’ assert status = expired, no tickets
Create order â†’ initiate payment â†’ simulate webhook failure â†’ assert order still pending, payment failed
Duplicate webhook â†’ assert idempotent (no double tickets)
Check-in valid ticket â†’ assert event_checkins row and ticket status = used
